thesis_status_reasoning: The system is performing exactly as designed under extreme asymmetric load conditions. Primary pumps have been sacrificed (0% load, cavitation sensor offline indicating shutdown) to protect infrastructure, while gravity reserves have seamlessly taken over to maintain 34 PSI in Sector 4—well above the 20 PSI contamination threshold. The 18,000 GPM flow through gravity alone, with 85% reserves remaining and surge relief valve properly closed, demonstrates the core thesis of resilient passive backup capability.
action_reasoning: System is viable and thesis-consistent, but reserve depletion rate must be tracked closely. At 18,000 GPM sustained draw, calculate time-to-critical (10% reserve threshold) and prepare primary pump restart protocols once demand stabilizes.

key_signals.signal: Sector 4 pressure maintained at 34 PSI with primary pumps offline
Gravity reserves at 85% while sustaining 18,000 GPM flow entirely through passive system
Primary pump load at 0% with cavitation sensor offline, indicating protective shutdown executed
System total flow at 18,000 GPM approaching but not exceeding 20,000 GPM leak threshold

biggest_uncertainty: Reserve depletion rate and duration of extreme demand—without time-series data, cannot determine if 85% reserve will sustain current 18,000 GPM draw long enough for primary systems to safely restart
